steps.title: Use the discriminant
steps.content: A quadratic has no real solutions when its discriminant is negative. For $-x^2+bx-64=0$, the coefficients are $a=-1$, $b=b$, and $c=-64$.

steps.title: Choose the least integer
steps.content: The integer values are greater than $-16$ and less than $16$, so the least possible integer is $-15$. The answer is -15.
